lines.text: At KuKClean (Jun–Jul 2024), set up product listings on the e-commerce storefront, optimizing descriptions and photographing catalog items for go-to-market launch.
Produced creative assets including packaging and social-media campaign visuals as a Product & Design Intern at KuKClean (Bangalore, Jun–Jul 2024).
Designed and evaluated Meridian, a system that produces due-diligence memos using parallel data sources such as yfinance, Wikipedia, Reddit JSON, and Google News RSS via asyncio.
Expertise in multi-agent orchestration systems and LLM evaluation with tools like Ollama, OpenAI API, and Anthropic API.
Proficient in tools such as n8n, FastAPI, and Postgres, with experience in server-side scripting and data management.

paragraphs: I am excited to apply for the Cybersecurity Research Intern position at Greycastle Security. As a student pursuing my Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science with a focus on multi-agent systems and AI, I have built projects that align closely with your tech stack and research culture. Specifically, my experience designing Meridian, an orchestration system using Python and FastAPI, has honed my skills in leveraging these tools to create robust security solutions. Additionally, the grounding evaluation aspect of Meridian mirrors Greycastle's commitment to producing verifiable and accurate threat intelligence reports.

At KuKClean, I gained hands-on experience with Figma for design tasks, which is a tool you utilize extensively at Greycastle. My role involved end-to-end product listings on e-commerce platforms, similar to the proof-of-concept exploits and documentation that your team produces. This experience has taught me the importance of meticulous attention to detail and the ability to quickly adapt to new tools and environments — essential skills for this internship.

I am particularly drawn to Greycastle’s focus on research-driven security work and its growing threat research team. My projects, such as Meridian and Anchor, reflect a deep commitment to creating systems where correctness is not just assumed but rigorously verified. I am eager to contribute to the development of cutting-edge cybersecurity tools and to learn from your innovative environment.
cover_letter.company_detail_referenced: Greycastle’s growing threat research team after several new enterprise contracts

linkedin.message: Hi — I'm applying for the Cybersecurity Research Intern role and am excited about your recent growth in threat research. My background in Python and hands-on work with Kali Linux aligns well with your tech stack. Looking forward to contributing to your team’s threat intelligence reports.
linkedin.company_detail_referenced: recent growth in threat research team

gaps.requirement: Experience with malware analysis or reverse engineering
gaps.how_to_close: Mention familiarity with relevant tools and techniques in the cover letter, emphasizing your interest in learning and applying these skills. Consider doing a focused weekend project related to malware analysis to showcase initiative.
Highlight your pursuit of cybersecurity education and interest in obtaining relevant certifications. Mention specific steps you are taking or plan to take, such as OSCP preparation.
